title: NO LIMITS Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law gives the President power to punish Chinese companies that help Russia's military by stopping them from doing business with America. The President can use these punishments on Chinese companies that work in Russia's defense industry or help make weapons for Russia. The law also makes government officials study whether big Chinese weapons makers should be punished too. It creates new rules to make sure these punishments also apply to smaller companies owned by the bigger ones.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the NO LIMITS Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings; Sense of Congress
sections.text:
Congress has found several important facts. First, on September thirtieth, two thousand twenty two, the Treasury Department put sanctions on a Chinese company called Sinno Electronics for helping Russia's military industry. Second, on January twenty sixth, two thousand twenty three, the Treasury Department sanctioned another Chinese company called Spacety China for supporting Russian military operations in Ukraine. Third, there is clear and growing proof that Chinese companies keep breaking American sanctions to help Russia's military. Fourth, the President has already found that China forces civilian companies to help its military, even though these companies appear to be private businesses. Fifth, because China helps Russia's military and it is hard to tell which Chinese companies are truly civilian, the United States needs new powers to protect national security.

Congress believes that China directly enables Russia's continued invasion of Ukraine. Therefore, it is time for the President to cut off more financing for Chinese companies that support Russia's military, stop Chinese companies involved in military improvements, put export controls on technology sent to China that might be sent to Russia, and sanction Chinese companies that export weapons and technology to Russia.

sections.title: Section 3 Imposition of Sanctions Relating to the People's Republic of China and Support for Russian Invasion of Ukraine
sections.text:
Starting ninety days after this law is passed, the President may put sanctions on foreign people or companies that meet certain conditions. The President can sanction those who are located in or organized under Chinese law and who operate in Russia's technology sector, defense sector, or other sectors chosen by the Treasury or State Secretary. The President can also sanction those who work for China's military or intelligence services and do harmful computer activities, make dual use technology or defense materials, or help people avoid American export controls or sanctions.

Starting one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the President may also sanction foreign people or companies that have business operations in Russia and are known Chinese military companies.

The sanction means the President will use emergency economic powers to block and prohibit all transactions involving the sanctioned person's property if that property is in the United States or comes under the control of an American person. The President can use the authorities from the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to carry out these sanctions. Anyone who violates these sanctions will face the same penalties as those who break the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.

There are some exceptions. These sanctions do not apply to intelligence and law enforcement activities that are properly reported to Congress or authorized by the United States. The sanctions also do not include the authority to stop importing goods, where goods means any physical items but not technical information.

The President may waive these sanctions for up to ninety days at a time if the President decides and tells Congress that the waiver is vital to American national interests.

This section defines several important terms. Business operations means participating in any commercial activity or organization. Foreign person means anyone who is not an American person. Knowingly means having actual knowledge or should have known. Person means an individual or organization. United States person means American citizens, permanent residents, organizations formed under American law, or people in the United States. Known Chinese military company means a specific list of fifty five Chinese companies including major technology and defense firms like Huawei, Aviation Industry Corporation of China, China Electronics Technology Group, and many others, plus any subsidiaries or related companies.

Nothing in this section limits the President's existing authority to designate or sanction people under executive orders or the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.

sections.title: Section 4 Determination of Sanctions on Arms Manufacturers of the People's Republic of China Engaged in Overseas Weapons Sales
sections.text:
Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Treasury Secretary must work with the State Secretary and Defense Secretary to submit a report to congressional committees. The report must determine whether each of eight specific Chinese arms manufacturing companies meets the criteria for sanctions described in Section three.

The appropriate congressional committees include the House committees on Foreign Affairs, Intelligence, and Strategic Competition with China, plus the Senate committees on Foreign Relations, Banking, and Intelligence.

The eight covered companies are China North Industries Group Corporation, Aviation Industry Corporation of China, China Electronics Technology Group Corporation, China South Industries Group Corporation, China Aerospace Science and Industry Corporation, China General Nuclear Power Group, China National Nuclear Corporation, and China State Shipbuilding Corporation.

sections.title: Section 5 Expanding Subsidiary Controls for PRC and Russian Entities to Prevent Diversion to Russia's War Effort
sections.text: Any licensing requirement that applies to a Chinese or Russian company because it is on the entity list will also apply to any subsidiary or other company that the listed company controls.

sections.title: Section 6 Definitions
sections.text: In this law, the word control has the same meaning as defined in section eight hundred point two zero eight of title thirty one of the Code of Federal Regulations, or any replacement regulations.

sections.title: Section 7 Regulations
sections.text: Within ninety days after this law is passed, the Commerce Secretary, Defense Secretary, and State Secretary must issue any regulations needed to carry out this law and its changes.
